title: Adaptive Gain PID DC Motor Speed Controller
description: Closed-loop DC motor speed regulator using hall-effect pulse counting, 3-region adaptive PID gain scheduling, MOSFET PWM drive, OLED live display, and SD card CSV logging with RPM fault shutdown.
objective: Regulate shaft RPM to setpoint within ±2% SS error; adapt gains per error region; log all states at 20 Hz; halt on out-of-range RPM.
constraints: Arduino Uno; 12 V motor ≤3 A; 50 ms control loop; microSD SPI CS=10; SSD1306 I2C.
supporting_fact_titles: Stepper Motor Closed-Loop Control via Magnetic Encoder Feedback
Adaptive Gain Scheduling for PID Controllers

element_usage_notes.Hall-Effect Interrupt Speed Sensor: Interrupt-driven pulse count provides RPM feedback each 50 ms loop tick.
element_usage_notes.IRLZ44N MOSFET PWM Motor Driver: Converts 0-255 PID output to effective motor voltage via analogWrite.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 128x32 OLED Status Panel: Live operator display of SP/RPM/PWM at 5 Hz.
element_usage_notes.FAT32 MicroSD CSV Logger: Persists full control state for post-run analysis.

element_interactions.description: PID converts RPM error to motor drive command each 50 ms.
Drive output changes shaft speed measured by hall sensor.
Current RPM shown on OLED at 5 Hz.
Control state logged to CSV at 20 Hz.
